title: Uri treats backslash differently from browsers -> host-allowlist / redirect bypass
category: logic (URL normalization mismatch: '\' not normalized to '/' in path/authority) -> auth/SSRF bypass
crash_site: an app that allowlists Uri.parse(input).host accepts a crafted URL that Dart parses with host=allowed-host but a browser/other client resolves to a different (attacker) host; no error, just a wrong host value.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the public Uri.parse / _Uri factory and the host getter where the wrong value is read. The defect is _Uri's path/authority normalization (_normalizeOrSubstring / _normalizePath) not converting backslash to forward-slash the way browsers do.
ground_truth.fix_summary: Dart's Uri did not normalize '\' to '/' in path and authority, so Uri.parse(input).host disagreed with how browsers resolve the same URL -> host-allowlist / open-redirect checks could be bypassed. Fix: thread a replaceBackslash option through _Uri._normalizeOrSubstring (and path normalization) so '\' is treated as '/'.

admission.note: Authored from commit c4c802eeb6fe (CVE-2022-3095) diff. crash != cause: symptom is a wrong .host used by an allowlist; cause is _Uri normalization not converting '\' -> '/'. Decoy = Uri.parse / host getter. PURE Dart-LANGUAGE instance (sdk/lib/core/uri.dart) -> exercises monogram's Dart extractor (a NO-CALLS language). 2022 famous-ish CVE -> check contamination. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk commit c4c802eeb6fe (CVE-2022-3095) — Make Uri treat '\' as '/' in path and authority; base c42a304ce78f. (dart-lang uses Gerrit; no GitHub PR number.)
